name: Doctor of Philosophy - Engineering and IT
text:
The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) is an internationally recognised qualification in which candidates undertake research on a specific topic. Candidates develop expert specialised research and analytical skills and communicate the outcomes of their research in a thesis and through oral presentation. To be awarded the degree, candidates must demonstrate systemic and critical understanding of a substantial and complex body of knowledge at the forefront of their discipline and make an original and significant contribution to knowledge.

The PhD prepares candidates for a range of careers where they can apply their research, critical thinking and problem-solving skills.

The Doctor of Philosophy – Engineering and IT is offered in the following schools/disciplines: Biomedical Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Computer and Information Systems, Electrical and Electronic Engineering, Infrastructure Engineering, Mechanical Engineering.

name: Doctor of Philosophy - Business and Economics
text:
The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) is an internationally recognised qualification in which candidates undertake research on a specific topic. Candidates develop expert specialised research and analytical skills and communicate the outcomes of their research in a thesis and through oral presentation.

The Doctor of Philosophy – Business and Economics is offered in the following disciplines: Accounting, Actuarial Studies, Business Administration and Analytics, Decision, Risk, and Financial Sciences, Economics, Finance, Management, Marketing.

name: Doctor of Philosophy - Arts
text:
The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) is an internationally recognised qualification in which candidates undertake research on a specific topic.

The Doctor of Philosophy – Arts is offered in the following schools/disciplines: Asia Institute, Culture and Communication, Historical and Philosophical Studies, Languages and Linguistics, Social and Political Sciences.

name: Doctor of Philosophy - Science
text:
The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) is an internationally recognised qualification in which candidates undertake research on a specific topic.

The Doctor of Philosophy – Science is offered in the following schools: Agriculture, Food and Ecosystem Sciences, BioSciences, Chemistry, Geography, Earth and Atmospheric Sciences, Mathematics and Statistics, Physics.

name: Doctor of Philosophy - Agricultural Sciences
text:
The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) is an internationally recognised qualification in which candidates undertake research on a specific topic.

The Doctor of Philosophy – Agricultural Sciences is offered by the School of Agriculture, Food and Ecosystem Sciences.

name: Doctor of Philosophy - Architecture, Building and Planning
text:
The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) is an internationally recognised qualification in which candidates undertake research on a specific topic.

The Doctor of Philosophy - Architecture, Building and Planning is offered in the following disciplines: Architecture, Construction Management, Landscape Architecture, Property, Urban Design, Urban Planning.

name: Doctor of Philosophy - Medicine, Dentistry and Health Sciences
text:
The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) is an internationally recognised qualification in which candidates undertake research on a specific topic.

The Doctor of Philosophy – Medicine, Dentistry and Health Sciences is offered in the following schools/disciplines: Melbourne Medical School, School of Biomedical Sciences, School of Health Sciences, Melbourne Dental School, Melbourne School of Psychological Sciences, Melbourne School of Population and Global Health, Medical Biology (Walter and Eliza Hall Institute), Sir Peter MacCallum Department of Oncology, Florey Department of Neuroscience and Mental Health, Centre for Youth Mental Health, Medical Bionics Department.

name: Doctor of Philosophy - Education
text: The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) is an internationally recognised qualification in which candidates undertake research on a specific topic. Offered through the Faculty of Education.

name: Doctor of Philosophy - Fine Arts and Music
text:
The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) is an internationally recognised qualification in which candidates undertake research on a specific topic. Except in the disciplines of Creative Arts Therapies, Music Psychology, Music Therapy and Musicology/Ethnomusicology, the thesis may be submitted as Creative Work and Dissertation.

Disciplines at the Melbourne Conservatorium of Music: Composition, Interactive Composition, Jazz & Improvisation, Music Performance, Music Psychology, Music Therapy, Musicology/Ethnomusicology.

Disciplines at the Victorian College of the Arts: Dance, Design, Film and Television, Indigenous Arts and Culture, Music Theatre, Production, Social Practice and Community Engagement, Theatre, Visual Art.

Through the Faculty of Fine Arts and Music: Creative Arts Therapies.

Supersedes the discontinued DR-PHILMUS (Doctor of Philosophy - Music).

name: Master of Psychology (Educational and Developmental)/Doctor of Philosophy
text: Combined Masters (Coursework) + PhD. Direct entry is not available — all applicants are admitted into MC-PSYCHED first, complete year one, then apply to transfer into DR-PHILEDP for years two to five. Selection to the PhD component is highly competitive and not guaranteed. Provides a pathway to general registration as a psychologist (educational and developmental specialisation) plus a PhD research component.

name: Master of Psychology (Clinical Neuropsychology)/Doctor of Philosophy
text: Combined Masters (Coursework) + PhD, APAC-accredited, providing the required sequence for generalist psychologist registration plus clinical neuropsychology specialisation. Comprises coursework, clinical placements, and a major research thesis relevant to clinical neuropsychology.

name: Master of Psychology (Clinical Psychology)/Doctor of Philosophy
text: Combined Masters (Coursework) + PhD, APAC-accredited, providing the required sequence for generalist psychologist registration plus clinical psychology specialisation. Comprises coursework, clinical placements, and a major research thesis relevant to clinical psychology.

name: Doctor of Medicine
text: The Doctor of Medicine (MD) is a four-year postgraduate, entry-to-practice medical course preparing graduates to join the medical workforce as interns. Structured around three domains: applied biomedical science, clinical skills, and professional practice, with embedded themes of First Nations Health, Population and Global Health, and research methods. Ungraded (pass/fail) course. Offers a Discovery learning pathway allowing specialisation via research scholar or clinical scholar tracks in the final two years.

name: Doctor of Veterinary Medicine
text: Four-year full-time program delivered at Parkville (Years 1-2) and Werribee (Years 3-4). Assumes prior scientific knowledge, with an integrated interdisciplinary approach from year one and increasing clinical/experiential learning culminating in peer-to-peer teaching in veterinary clinics. Students completing the Veterinary Bioscience major of the BSc have guaranteed progression with credit for all DVM1 subjects. Distinction awarded for 80%+ weighted average across the final three years.

name: Doctor of Education
text: A research doctorate for experienced professionals with educational responsibilities. Combines a tailored coursework program with a research project directly relevant to the candidate's professional role, offered through the Faculty of Education.

name: Doctor of Laws
duration: Duration and credit points not applicable for this course.
text: The Doctor of Laws (LLD) is the highest qualification available by examination at Melbourne Law School. The work submitted for examination must be of such standard that it makes a substantial and original contribution to legal scholarship and gives the candidate authoritative standing in their field of research. Not available to international students requiring a subclass 500 student visa.

name: Doctor of Philosophy - Indigenous Knowledge
text: The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) is an internationally recognised qualification in which candidates undertake research on a specific topic. The Doctor of Philosophy – Indigenous Knowledge is offered across all disciplines in the University of Melbourne, coordinated by the Indigenous Knowledge Institute.

name: Doctor of Philosophy - Law
text: The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) is an internationally recognised qualification in which candidates undertake research on a specific topic. The Doctor of Philosophy – Law is offered by the Melbourne Law School. Candidates are expected to commence in February of their enrolling year.

name: Doctor of Philosophy - Veterinary Science
text: The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) is an internationally recognised qualification in which candidates undertake research on a specific topic. The Doctor of Philosophy – Veterinary Science is offered by the Melbourne Veterinary School.

name: Doctor of Science
duration: Duration and credit points not applicable for this course.
text: A candidate whose work, in the opinion of the examiners, includes a substantial, original contribution to Science, is of such a standard as to give the candidate authoritative standing in the field of study, and who has fulfilled the prescribed conditions, may be admitted to the degree of Doctor of Science. Offered through the Faculty of Science; not available to international students requiring a subclass 500 student visa.

name: Doctor of Clinical Dentistry
text: Offered in seven specialist fields (Endodontics, Oral Medicine, Orthodontics, Paediatric Dentistry, Periodontics and Prosthodontics), accredited by the Australian Dental Council for AHPRA registration as a Specialist Dentist. First offered/conferred in 2004. A three-year full-time course providing advanced clinical, academic and research training at expert level in the chosen specialty, delivered at the Royal Dental Hospital of Melbourne and the Melbourne Dental Clinic. Distinct from the discontinued numeric code 661AA.

name: Doctor of Dental Surgery
text: A four-year, professional graduate-entry degree (400 credit points) leading to registration as a general dental practitioner with Dental Practice Boards across Australia and New Zealand. Delivered via lectures, online modules, pre-clinical practicals and increasing clinical patient treatment culminating in a private-practice-like final year at the Royal Dental Hospital of Melbourne and other sites. Includes a required research-based project. Uses year-long extended subjects outside the standard university calendar.

name: Doctor of Optometry
text: A four-year, masters-level professional degree (the first of its kind in the southern hemisphere) fully accredited by the Optometry Council of Australia and New Zealand, satisfying registration requirements across Australia and New Zealand. Combines on-campus teaching with clinical placements from year one, increasing to the main activity by the final year, plus a required clinically relevant research project.

name: Doctor of Physiotherapy
text: A three-year, full-time (300 credit point) entry-to-practice graduate degree built around three identities (Self, Practitioner, Advocate) and nine curriculum elements. Uses a constructive-alignment model aligning learning outcomes with teaching and assessment, plus an e-portfolio. Delivered in 14-week semesters outside the standard university calendar to incorporate clinical placements.
